site.title: ax — TUI API Client
site.description: A fast, beautiful terminal-based HTTP client inspired by Postman/Insomnia, powered by Bubble Tea.

headline: The API Client
Built for Your Terminal
hero.subtitle: ax brings xh/httpie-style syntax parsing into a blazing-fast, interactive Terminal User Interface (TUI). Written in Go, powered by Bubble Tea.

features.title: Three-Pane TUI
features.description: Sidebar, request builder, and response viewer — cycle between them with Tab. Powered by Bubble Tea v2 for buttery-smooth terminal rendering.

features.title: xh/Httpie Syntax
features.description: Intuitive single-line syntax parsing right from your prompt. Type :8080/api/users name==John and go.

features.title: Chroma Highlighting
features.description: Beautiful Catppuccin Macchiato-themed JSON highlighting powered by Chroma v2. Responses are colorized and formatted for readability.

features.title: SQLite History
features.description: Every request is persisted locally via SQLite with WAL mode. Your history survives restarts — browse, reload, or delete with Ctrl+D.

features.title: Clipboard + Copy
features.description: Press Ctrl+Y to copy any response body to your clipboard. Works on X11, Wayland, and macOS — zero configuration.

features.title: Cross-Platform
features.description: Linux, macOS, Windows — one statically linked binary. No CGo, no dependencies. Install via Homebrew, AUR, or download directly from GitHub Releases.

tabs.label: Arch Linux
tabs.description: Available via ax-cli in the Arch User Repository
tabs.note: Also available via paru -S ax-cli or manual PKGBUILD install from AUR.

tabs.label: Standalone
tabs.description: One-liner auto-detects your OS and architecture
tabs.note: Works on Linux, macOS. Detects your OS, architecture, and preferred package manager automatically.

tabs.label: PowerShell
tabs.description: Run in PowerShell 5.1+ or PowerShell Core
tabs.note: Downloads the Windows binary from GitHub Releases, extracts it, and adds it to your PATH.

scripts.description: Our intelligent installer automatically detects your platform and picks the right installation method. Fully open source — audit it before you pipe it.
